description:
You are acting as a release-manager who must prepare a set of
    service configuration files for the next production deployment.
    All the material you need is already present in

        /home/user/deployment_configs/

    Detailed objectives
    -------------------
    1. Inside that directory there are exactly four “*.conf” files.  
       Every file follows *precisely* this key-value format (1 value per
       line, no extra whitespace):

            ServiceName: <string>
            Version: <major>.<minor>.<patch>
            Environment: <string>
            Status: <string>

    2. A service that currently has

            Environment: staging
            Status: READY

       must be promoted to production:
          • increment ONLY the patch component of Version by **+1**  
            (e.g. 1.3.7  →  1.3.8)  
          • replace Environment with «production»  
          • replace Status      with «LIVE»

       Every other service must be left completely untouched.

    3. Produce the following two *new* artefacts in the same directory
       (exact filenames, exact spellings):

       a)  /home/user/deployment_configs/release_summary.csv  
           CSV with **exactly three columns** in this order:
               Environment,Status,Count
           One header line plus one data line per
           Environment/Status combination that exists *after* your
           updates.  Sort rows first by Environment, then by Status,
           both ascending.  Do not add extra spaces.  Example pattern:

               Environment,Status,Count
               production,LIVE,3
               staging,IN_TEST,1

       b)  /home/user/deployment_configs/release_notes.txt  
           Fixed-width notes that will be e-mailed:

               Line 1 : literal text  "Release Notes - 2023-10-15"
               Line 2 : exactly 26 ‘=’ characters
               Line 3 : blank
               Subsequent lines: one line per *promoted* service,
                               sorted alphabetically by ServiceName,
                               format  "<ServiceName> <Version>"
               Final file must end with a single trailing newline.

    4. Create a simple text log capturing each promotion:

           /home/user/deployment_configs/change_log.log

       One line per promoted service, format **exactly**:

           <ServiceName> bumped <oldVersion> -> <newVersion>

       Maintain alphabetical order by ServiceName.

    5. IMPORTANT CONSTRAINTS  
       • Use only standard CLI tools (awk, sed, cut, sort, etc.).  
       • Do NOT hard-code absolute version strings—derive them from
         the files you read.  
       • All paths, filenames and output formats must match this
         description byte-for-byte; the automated grader will fail on
         any deviation.  
       • Do your work exclusively inside /home/user/deployment_configs/ ;
         you do not need elevated privileges.  
       • Display whatever console output you find helpful, but make sure
         the artefacts listed above exist with the required contents
         when you are done.

        (content exactly as initial)

    Unchanged file: /home/user/deployment_configs/service_delta.conf
        (content exactly as initial)

    Generated file: /home/user/deployment_configs/release_summary.csv

        production,LIVE,3
        staging,IN_TEST,1

    Generated file: /home/user/deployment_configs/release_notes.txt

        GammaDB 0.9.13

        (note: final newline directly after "0.9.13")

    Generated file: /home/user/deployment_configs/change_log.log
